title: SSRF, XML, Content, Query, Abuse, Secrets, and Retention Defense · Systems Transfer
summary: An independent community archive receives a different publisher mix, malformed feed, validator state, tenant, schedule, database condition, accessibility need, platform, and injected failure and transfers ssrf, xml, content, query, abuse, secrets, and retention defense into a changed threat-modeled feed-service control set. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of threat-modeled feed-service control set with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through p…
objectives: Trace assets, actors, trust boundaries, entry points, abuse cases, controls, detection, response, and residual risk across every data flow.
Combine URL grammar, allow or deny policy, address validation, redirect checks, proxy controls, egress isolation, metadata blocking, and rebinding tests.
Align wire, decompression, XML, text, HTML sanitation, search, pagination, bulk, and export limits so one stage cannot amplify another.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Go Threat Model
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go team handles case 6067 during a PostgreSQL claim and failover drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove defense-in-depth SSRF, parser and query budgets, secrets, retention, deletion, and residual ownership; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant operations must preserve the prior admitted state; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, compression, entity, depth, timeout, or publisher-budget failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Go Threat Model.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace assets, actors, trust boundaries, entry points, abuse cases, controls, detection, response, and residual risk across every data flow. For Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Go Threat Model, keep Feed Go Threat Model connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.
